rationale: The cited paper is not just listed in background references; it is used in multiple functional places in the citing paper. The citing authors rely on Houston and Johnson (2000) to support their dependence/holdup argument in vertical alliances and include it in their structured review of prior single-partner alliance studies. They also explicitly follow its measurement approach when operationalizing upstream firm reputation via Fortune rankings. These uses indicate a meaningful conceptual and empirical setup role.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Houston and Johnson (2000) as prior governance evidence in buyer-supplier alliances and uses it to justify dependence-related arguments. It also applies the cited paper's reputation operationalization approach in its own event-study model.
